fresh_facts.claim: Ali Khamenei, Iran's previous supreme leader, died on February 28, 2026, and was succeeded by Mojtaba Khamenei.
Israel claimed it killed Iranian Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib in an overnight strike on March 18, 2026.
Ali Larijani, one of Iran's highest-ranking officials, died on March 17, 2026, with his funeral held in Tehran.
Iranian missile strikes in retaliation for Israeli strikes killed two people near Tel Aviv on March 17, 2026.
Judiciary chief Gholamhossein Mohseni Ejei, President Masoud Pezeshkian, and Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf vowed continuity and revenge in public statements following leadership losses.
Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi stated that Iran's system remained structurally strong regardless of individual losses and that Iran's doctrine against nuclear weapons was unlikely to change.

U.S. Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard told the Senate Intelligence Committee on March 18 that Iran's government 'appears to be intact, but largely degraded due to attacks' while retaining ability to strike U.S. and allied interests.
Strikes hit Iran's South Pars gas complex and nearby facilities at Asaluyeh on March 18, 2026.
Iran halted gas flows to Iraq to divert supply to domestic use following strikes on South Pars.
Brent crude surged above $108 and toward $110 in response to escalation on March 18, 2026.
The Revolutionary Guards announced a new 'Wave 59' salvo of missiles and drones.
U.N. Secretary-General António Guterres called for 'the war in the Middle East must stop' and urged full implementation of Security Council resolutions.
Saudi Arabia convened ministers from Arab and Islamic states in Riyadh for emergency consultations regarding the Middle East conflict.
NATO deployed additional Patriot systems to Turkey to reinforce defenses against missile threats.
Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi called for a new Strait of Hormuz protocol after weeks of disruption to one of the world's most important energy corridors.

The National Council of Resistance of Iran (NCRI) announced a detailed plan to establish a transitional government with a six-month roadmap to transfer power through free elections in the event of the regime's fall.
A third U.S. carrier, USS George H. W. Bush, was sent to the Middle East on March 7, 2026.
US Central Command reported over 7,800 targets hit in Iran as of early March 2026.
US-Israeli combined forces struck Iranian military sites on March 15-17, 2026, including airbases in Dezful and Bandar Abbas, missile production near Tehran, and IRGC Navy depot in Bushehr.
Israeli airstrikes killed senior Iranian officials Basij chief Gholamreza Soleimani on early March 17, 2026.
Israeli forces conducted a ground invasion of southern Lebanon following Iranian retaliation on March 17, 2026.
Israel and the US struck Iranian drone facilities in South Khorasan, airbases, missile sites, and IRGC facilities across six or more provinces on March 15-16, 2026.
Hundreds of strikes across 26 Iranian provinces degraded air defense systems (200 systems), missile capabilities, leadership facilities, IRGC bases, and detention centers including Mariwan.
US and Israeli intelligence reported Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei was wounded as of March 15, 2026.
IRGC Aerospace Force claimed first Sejjil missile launch at Israel on March 15, 2026.
Iran launched over 500 ballistic and naval missiles and 2,000 drones since February 28, 2026, as of March 5.
Iran shifted to high-impact strikes using missiles and drones of 1,000kg or greater by March 10, 2026.
Kataib Hezbollah conducted drone attacks on ex-US Victory Base on March 14, 2026.
Harakat Ansar Allah al Awfiya suffered losses to Israeli strikes in al Qaim on March 15, 2026.
IRGC ordered evacuation of Mariwan on March 3, 2026, possibly in preparation for ground operations.

regime_fall.current_status: The Iranian regime faces an unprecedented convergence of crises following Mojtaba Khamenei's succession on February 28: cascading leadership decapitation through coordinated US-Israeli strikes across 26 provinces (7,800+ targets, 200 air defense systems destroyed), catastrophic damage to energy infrastructure (South Pars complex, Kharg Island, Asaluyeh facilities), and material military degradation despite continuing asymmetric retaliation. The new leadership's defiant posturing masks severe structural damage, with senior officials killed (Khatib, Larijani, Soleimani), over 500 ballistic missiles and 2,000 drones expended since late February, and domestic energy diversion measures indicating acute economic strain.
regime_fall.last_major_event: US and Israeli forces executed comprehensive multi-day strikes on March 15-19, 2026, targeting Iranian military command structures, air defense systems, missile production, IRGC bases, and energy infrastructure across 26 provinces, killing senior officials including Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib, Security Chief Ali Larijani, and Basij Chief Gholamreza Soleimani, while Brent crude surged above $108 per barrel and a third US carrier was deployed to the region.
